comment.body:
the key, what i want you to understand is, to always have a plan!
this is the most important.
have a read on the tape first, read through the analysis, read through the positioning, understand what the certain zones/lvls mean, whats their significance, and what you need to mind at them during the session.

it is based on your trading style.
but take a paper,
determine how much money you are willing to put into work on that day, what is the amount of money, you want to put into risk on that tape you estimated.
then put it on the paper that "at this lvl i'm gonna do this, and i need to watch that... etc."
and lets be sticked to it.

comment.body:
if it doesn't materialize, then just simply stay out.
you either do what you pre-decided, or do nothing.

but if you fomo in, and you lose, you will be emotional, and will start to chase it to take back you lose...
and this is the point when Wall Street owns you!
you are not a human anymore, just a toy in their dirty hands.
and you give them free money.

comment.body:
so if i would be allowed to advise anything, i would say: do this what i wrote just right above here.
and also determine how much you are willing to lose, and what is you daily profit target.
once any of them is hit, close the position. no matter what. 

and this way, you will be better and better trader day by day.
you will see it.
and this is how you can train yourself to gain controll over your trading behavior

comment.body:
i provide you my lvls, my description of the positioning, my rough read, and an indicator to
1) give you a context,
2) give you the lvls that actually determine the characteristics, and that actually have meanings
3) explain the characteristics
4) through the momentum indicator, you get probabilities during the day. 
(CMik's recommended setup is actually pretty good)

And all you need to do is to determine the amount of money you are willing to put into work on that tape that day (be rational, take your weekly/monthly spendings and incomes into account), 
determine your plan,
determine your daily profit and loss targets,
lets be sticked to it, no matter what.

And you will avoid to get owned.

lets have a little book/diary on your desk, and write all your daily plans down.

comment.body:
I thought it was very interesting today when trump event started we had almost the exact opposite market behaviour from the failed breakdown that trump talk caused on Friday. 

On Friday it was a big sudden jump down, looking like market would crater, pulling shorts in. Then huge reversal. Today we had a big sudden jump up looking like a nice break out only for the exact opposite to happen. Good pattern to recognise for this trumpian market. I left VERY quick money on the table twice, and I’ll try my damn hardest not to do the same again.

comment.body: quick note regarding the pattern I've been commenting on.  Upon reflection, it may have ended today.  usually the 5th leg is the longest, but here the 3rd and 4th were exceptionally long and deep.  Concept of alteration.  the 5th then would be more similar is dept to legs 1 and 2.  If so, it probably completed today.  off to sleep.

comment.body: Also, remembered an I.V. indicator I used to use on my ToS charts. Not sure if it still applies after Alma talking about seeing deviations due to outside anomalies. Someone mentioned I.V. increasing on the brk today, and that is what is showing on this indicator, but if anyone wants to add it to try it out: Studies->Edit Studies->type 'vol'->add 'ImpVolatility'.

comment.body:
now, the skews doesn't really change on the put wing into the opexes.
for the week, it shows strong bid during the whole week on the put side. tomorrow especially.. but also the tail calls start to move. this is more vol trading + donwside hedging activity, so market keeps expecting downside pressures withouth tail-event fear, so the spot decay narrative remains
